Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. U+237C ⍼ Is Azimuth 279 upvotes, 26 comments
  2. Tony Hoare has died 1756 upvotes, 225 comments
  3. Mesh over Bluetooth LE, TCP, or Reticulum 89 upvotes, 8 comments
  4. Julia Snail – An Emacs Development Environment for Julia Like Clojure's Cider 60 upvotes, 7 comments
  5. Writing my own text editor, and daily-driving it 93 upvotes, 22 comments
  6. Pike: To Exit or Not to Exit 20 upvotes, 2 comments
  7. Zig – Type Resolution Redesign and Language Changes 189 upvotes, 59 comments
  8. TADA: Fast, Reliable Speech Generation Through Text-Acoustic Synchronization 26 upvotes, 4 comments
  9. Standardizing source maps 26 upvotes, 4 comments
  10. AutoKernel: Autoresearch for GPU Kernels 14 upvotes, 1 comments
  11. Universal vaccine against respiratory infections and allergens 233 upvotes, 78 comments
  12. FFmpeg-over-IP – Connect to remote FFmpeg servers 180 upvotes, 58 comments
  13. SSH Secret Menu 187 upvotes, 68 comments
  14. Cloudflare crawl endpoint 281 upvotes, 113 comments
  15. Create value for others and don’t worry about the returns 273 upvotes, 119 comments
  16. EQT eyes potential $6B sale of Linux pioneer SUSE, sources say 49 upvotes, 18 comments
  17. Surpassing vLLM with a Generated Inference Stack 36 upvotes, 12 comments
  18. Levels of Agentic Engineering 166 upvotes, 84 comments
  19. Building a TB-303 from Scratch 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  20. Launch HN: RunAnywhere (YC W26) – Faster AI Inference on Apple Silicon 209 upvotes, 129 comments
  21. Meta acquires Moltbook 486 upvotes, 325 comments
  22. Debian decides not to decide on AI-generated contributions 324 upvotes, 252 comments
  23. Yann LeCun raises $1B to build AI that understands the physical world 453 upvotes, 370 comments
  24. RISC-V Is Sloooow 229 upvotes, 216 comments
  25. Agents that run while I sleep 322 upvotes, 335 comments
  26. Launch HN: Didit (YC W26) – Stripe for Identity Verification 67 upvotes, 59 comments
  27. Roblox is minting teen millionaires 130 upvotes, 135 comments
  28. I'm going to build my own OpenClaw, with blackjack and bun 25 upvotes, 19 comments
  29. Support for Aquantia AQC113 and AQC113C Ethernet Controllers on FreeBSD 7 upvotes, 3 comments
  30. Why does AI tell you to use Terminal so much? 21 upvotes, 26 comments

Last generated 5:00pm, 11 March 2026. Source here.
